ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT F.G. NORMAN & SONS' MOTION TO QUASH SUMMONS. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: GRANTED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT BOTH CCP 416.10 AND CCP 416.20 ARE NOT MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE AND COULD BOTH APPLY TO A SUSPENDED CORPORATION, PLAINTIFFS' SERVICE ON MS. NORMAN FAILED TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF EITHER SECTION. CORPORATIONS CODE 2011(B), CITED IN CCP 416.20(B), IS INAPPLICABLE BECAUSE THAT SECTION PERTAINS EXCLUSIVELY TO DISSOLVED CORPORATIONS. A SUSPENDED CORPORATION DOES NOT, MERELY BY VIRTUE OF ITS SUSPENSION AND SUBSEQUENT INACTIVITY, BECOME A DISSOLVED CORPORATION. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT F.G. NORMAN & SONS IS A DISSOLVED CORPORATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7707.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Josette Johnson, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), specially appearing for defendant / moving party F.G. Norman & Sons

Christopher Hersom, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Fredrick J. Smith, et al.

RULING:
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially appearing defendant F.G. Norman & Sons' motion to quash summons is granted. Assuming without deciding that both CCP 416.10 and CCP 416.20 are not mutually exclusive and could both apply to a suspended corporation, plaintiffs' service on Ms. Norman failed to meet the requirements of either section. Corporations Code 2011(b), cited in CCP 416.20(b), is inapplicable because that section pertains exclusively to dissolved corporations. A suspended corporation does not, merely by virtue of its suspension and subsequent inactivity, become a dissolved corporation. There is no evidence that F.G. Norman & Sons is a dissolved corporation. Order signed in open court. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7707. = (220/HEK)
